summary: Gruber reviews the HomePod Mini as a straightforward, positive product that delivers exactly what Apple customers have been asking for: a much cheaper HomePod that still sounds great. He finds the $100 speaker sounds remarkable for its size, works well both solo and as a stereo pair, and includes all standard HomePod features except Apple TV home theater support. He argues it may be the nicest device Apple has ever made at the $100 price point, noting that it's one-third the price of a full-size HomePod but far more than one-third the quality. The review is notably concise, matching the product's simplicity.
key_points: The HomePod Mini sounds remarkably good for its size and $100 price, though predictably not as good as the $300 full-size HomePod
Two paired Minis sound more than twice as good as one, doing sophisticated audio processing beyond simple left/right channel splitting
At $100, it may be the nicest device Apple has ever made at that price point, with the included 20W power adapter alone worth $20
The Mini is a full HomePod in every way except Apple TV home theater features like Dolby Atmos and surround sound
Gruber identifies two buyer profiles: existing HomePod owners expanding to more rooms, and price-conscious newcomers who should stop worrying about quality at this price
money_quotes: The HomePod Mini is a delightful smart speaker, and a good value at $100.
I wouldn't hold my breath waiting for Apple to make a $50 anything.
The HomePod Mini might be the nicest device Apple has ever made for $100.
Two paired Minis — again, just like with the full-size version — sound more than twice as good.
HomePod Mini is one-third the price of a regular HomePod but way more than one-third the quality.
It's really quite a thing to hear, and they're clearly doing more than splitting audio into left/right channels.
key_insight: The HomePod Mini is exactly the product everyone asked Apple to make — a genuinely good speaker at a genuinely accessible price point, delivering far more than one-third the quality at one-third the cost of the original.
